summaries.content: This lesson explores how different months of the year are busy with festivals, activities, and special events. In India, every month brings something exciting! January has Makar Sankranti, Pongal, Lohri, and Republic Day. People fly kites, eat til-gur sweets, and watch the grand parade. The lesson shows how festivals bring families and communities together. Children learn about the calendar and how to read which month, date, and day it is.
keyPoints: January is a very busy month with many festivals and Republic Day
Makar Sankranti is celebrated with kite flying and til-gur sweets
Pongal and Lohri are also January harvest festivals
A calendar helps us keep track of dates and events

summaries.content: This lesson looks at festivals and celebrations across different months of the year. Holi in March brings colours and joy, Baisakhi in April celebrates the wheat harvest in Punjab, Eid and Raksha Bandhan come in different months each year, and Diwali lights up homes in October or November. The lesson highlights how India is a land of diverse cultures and traditions, and different communities celebrate different festivals. Every festival has its own special food, clothing, songs, and rituals.
keyPoints: India celebrates festivals in every month of the year
Holi is the festival of colours celebrated in March
Harvest festivals like Baisakhi celebrate the gathering of crops
Diwali is the festival of lights with diyas and sweets

summaries.content: This lesson connects festivals and months to seasons and weather. Summer months (April-June) bring mangoes, holidays, and festivals like Akshaya Tritiya. The monsoon months (July-September) bring rain, greenery, and festivals like Teej and Onam. Winter months (November-February) bring cold weather, woollen clothes, and festivals like Christmas and Lohri. Children learn that the activities we do, the clothes we wear, and the food we eat all change with the seasons. National days like Independence Day (August 15) and Gandhi Jayanti (October 2) are also important.
keyPoints: Activities and clothing change with the seasons
Summer, monsoon, and winter each have their own festivals
National holidays are celebrated by the whole country together
Weather affects what we eat, wear, and do each month
